title.title: Threshold SDK
title.subtitle: The TypeScript SDK for building AI that helps people know what they actually think

problem.title: The Gap Between AI Output and Human Meaning
problem.body:
Most AI tools optimize for engagement or task completion. But there's a deeper problem: people often don't know what they value until they're forced to confront tradeoffs.

Threshold SDK is built for the apps that live in that gap — tools that help users articulate, refine, and act on their own values through structured AI interaction.
bullets: Generic AI responses flatten nuance instead of revealing it
Most SDKs treat values as a preference toggle, not a first-class concept
The hard problem: getting from 'I want X' to 'I understand why I want X'

audience-branch.title: Where Are You Coming From?
audience-branch.subtitle: Threshold works differently depending on your vantage point

branches.label: I build things
branches.desc: Show me the API, types, and how this fits in my stack

branches.label: I make decisions
branches.desc: Tell me the value prop and what this enables for my product

branches.label: I think about ideas
branches.desc: Walk me through the philosophy and what makes this different

dev-intro.title: Built for TypeScript-First Teams
dev-intro.body: Threshold SDK is a TypeScript-native library with full type safety, tree-shakeable modules, and a composable architecture. It ships with adapters for major inference providers and a core abstraction layer that keeps your app logic decoupled from the model.
bullets: Full TypeScript types across all APIs — no any leakage
Provider-agnostic: swap models without rewriting your pipeline
Structured output primitives for values elicitation and perspective modeling
Composable: use just the modules you need
dev-intro.code: import { ThresholdClient, ElicitValues } from '@threshold-labs/sdk'; const client = new ThresholdClient({ apiKey: process.env.THRESHOLD_KEY }); const session = await client.createSession({ mode: 'values-elicitation' }); // session.values, session.tradeoffs, session.profile — all typed

pm-intro.title: What Threshold Unlocks for Your Product
pm-intro.body: The most successful AI products don't just answer users — they help users understand themselves better. Threshold SDK is the infrastructure layer for that category of product.
bullets: Differentiate on depth: move beyond chatbots to genuine personalization
Capture preference signal that actually predicts behavior, not just stated intent
Build trust loops where users feel heard, not processed
Modular enough to layer into existing products without a full rewrite

vision-intro.title: The Thesis Behind Threshold
vision-intro.body:
The premise: most people don't know what they value until they're forced to reason about tradeoffs in a safe, scaffolded environment. AI can be that scaffold — but only if it's designed to clarify rather than answer for.

Threshold is built on the insight that values elicitation, perspective simulation, and structured reflection are learnable computational patterns — not just human skills. The SDK gives developers the primitives to build that kind of software.

core-concepts.title: Three Core Primitives
core-concepts.body: Everything in Threshold is built from three composable primitives that can be combined into any values-aware application:
bullets: Elicitation — structured questioning sequences that surface latent preferences without leading the witness
Perspective modeling — simulate how a given value system filters information (the 'societies' pattern)
Graph navigation — traverse a user's value graph to find tensions, alignments, and blind spots

use-case-branch.title: What Does This Look Like in Practice?
use-case-branch.subtitle: Pick the use case closest to what you're building

branches.label: Product and consumer apps
branches.desc: Onboarding flows, personalization engines, preference capture

branches.label: Research and enterprise tools
branches.desc: Decision support, stakeholder alignment, organizational values mapping

use-case-consumer.title: Consumer Apps: From Preference to Profile
use-case-consumer.body: Traditional onboarding asks users what they want. Threshold-powered onboarding discovers what they value — and builds a living model that improves with every interaction.
bullets: Adaptive questioning that feels like conversation, not a survey
Profile object that tracks value clusters, not just feature flags
Tradeoff resolution: when user goals conflict, surface the tension explicitly
Privacy-respecting: values graph lives client-side or in your own store

use-case-enterprise.title: Enterprise: Alignment at Scale
use-case-enterprise.body: Organizational decisions fail when stakeholders aren't aligned on underlying values — not just stated goals. Threshold SDK enables tools that make that alignment visible and actionable.
bullets: Stakeholder values mapping across teams and roles
Tension detection: identify where value systems conflict before they become blockers
Decision scaffolding: structure choices so tradeoffs are explicit and documented
Audit trail of value reasoning, not just outcomes

getting-started.title: Getting Started in Three Steps
getting-started.body: Threshold SDK is designed to be incrementally adoptable — you don't need to commit to the full mental model on day one. Start with one primitive and expand from there.
bullets: Install: npm install @threshold-labs/sdk (TypeScript types included)
Read DEVELOPERS.md for architecture decisions and provider setup
Pick one primitive — Elicitation is the gentlest entry point — and wire it to your existing flow
getting-started.code: # Install with npm npm install @threshold-labs/sdk # Or with bun bun add @threshold-labs/sdk

close.title: Build AI That Reveals, Not Just Responds
